channel.title: Upper Kirby Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Upper Kirby Events

item.title: Free Watch Party: USA vs Bosnia & Herzegovina
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 5:30 PM

The Round of 32 rolls on, and the field keeps getting smaller. Every pass, every save, every goal carries the weight of a nation tonight. Come celebrate the beautiful game with us as more World Cup dreams are made and broken. Festival gates open at 5:30 p.m. come early an enjoy the sounds of DJ Athenz. Kickoff is at 7:00 p.m. Admission is free! Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Visit Smalls Sliders for burgers, fries, and shakes and cool down with Frios Gourmet Pops. Stop by Hearsay’s festival tent for a cold beer or beer bucket or grab a michelada, wine, or beer from Gaspacho’s festival tent. Non-alcoholic drinks from Coca-Cola as well as pre-packaged snacks can be purchased at the Community Partner Concessions Booth, proceeds benefiting Levy Park and the event’s community partners. Community Partner: We are proud to be joined by our Community Partner, Dynamo & Dash Charities, for this event! By leveraging the passion of players, staff, and fans, Dynamo & Dash Charities provides resources and education to positively impact lives in the community. Stop by the Community Partner Booth to support their amazing work and learn more about their initiatives. All food & beverage proceeds from the Community Partner booth will support Dynamo & Dash Charities and Levy Park. Face Painting & Henna Art: Get into the tournament spirit with face and body art from Gio’s Art Kreations and Henna Station & Glitter Bar Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds. Festival Guidelines: VISITORS MUST COMPLY WITH DIRECTIONS FROM PARK PERSONNEL, OR THEY WILL BE ASKED TO LEAVE THE PARK. PARENTS ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THEIR CHILDREN’S SAFETY IN THE PARK. CHAIRS AND SEATING: No outside furniture. Blankets may be brought into the Festival site for the designated blanket seating area. All other furniture is available on a first-come, first-served basis. STROLLERS & WAGONS: Oversized strollers and wagons may need to be parked in a designated area during the festival. All visitors must comply with park personnel regarding stroller and wagon parking. FOOD AND BEVERAGES: Guests are permitted to enter with one factory-sealed bottle of water. Outside food and drinks are prohibited (exceptions will be made for baby food and necessary medical dietary food items). A variety of food, non-alcoholic drinks, beer, and wine will be available for purchase from Festival concessionaires. RE-ENTRY: If you leave the Festival site, you will be re-screened before re-entry. THE FOLLOWING ARE PROHIBITED: Smoking or Vaping Umbrellas Drug Use Kites and Drones Recreational Motorized Vehicles Guns, knives, any weaponry Soliciting Bikes, scooters, rollerblades, and roller skates

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Zumba by Tiny Fitness
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 6 PM

Join the party and sweat with Tiny Fitness to rhythms from around the world! This hour-long Zumba fitness class will have you grooving, shaking, and toning to salsa, hip hop, samba, and bhangra beats – and so many more. Open to all fitness levels and recommended for ages 16+, no equipment needed. Always move to your own beat and consult your health team before beginning or modifying your exercise program. To learn more about the Tiny Fitness studio visit their website.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Slow Flow with Stephanie McCarrey
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 7 PM

This is an all-levels yoga flow for beginners to experts that will help you build strength and mobility. The vinyasa flow is set to the beat of the latest top 40 and throwback favorites. The last portion of class will be all about opening the body and increasing mobility.  Each class will end with a sound bath Shavasana.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Irish Session
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

From McGonigel's Mucky Duck

Event details

item.title: Children’s Bingo
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Bring the kiddos and join our Levy Park team for Children’s Bingo! This 30-minute program, ideal for kids aged 4 to 12, is a fun way for children to practice their letters and numbers while earning prizes. Levy Park provides Bingo cards, markers, and prizes. B-I-N-G-O!

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Blue Dragon Tai Chi
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Join us at Levy Park with Taoist Healing for Tai Chi and Meditation every Friday at 9 AM and every Thursday 6 PM. Practice Tai Chi to connect and focus the body and mind with slow, low-impact gentle movements and stretches. Open to all ages and levels, this class is great for those who want to de-stress, find their inner peace, and increase overall health.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Join us at Levy Park with Taoist Healing for Tai Chi and Meditation every Thursday at 6 pm and Friday at 9 am. Practice Tai Chi to connect and focus the body and mind with slow, low-impact gentle movements and stretches. Open to all ages and levels, this class is great for those who want to de-stress, find their inner peace, and increase overall health.

From Levy Park Houston

item.title: Zumba by Tiny Fitness
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 10 AM

Join the party and sweat with Tiny Fitness to rhythms from around the world! This hour-long Zumba fitness class will have you grooving, shaking, and toning to salsa, hip hop, samba, and bhangra beats – and so many more. Open to all fitness levels and recommended for ages 16+, no equipment needed. Always move to your own beat and consult your health team before beginning or modifying your exercise program. To learn more about the Tiny Fitness studio visit their website.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Urban Harvest Farmers Market
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8 AM

Location: 2752 Buffalo Speedway, Houston, TX 77098

Nonprofit Saturday farmers market with local produce, food, and garden vendors.

From Urban Harvest Farmers Market

Event details

item.title: Young Audiences of Houston
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Young Audiences of Houston offers family friendly, live art experiences perfect for a wide variety of ages. These interactive workshops and performances last between 60 and 90 minutes and involve YAH artists and ensembles, crossing genres of dance, visual arts, theater, music, and literary arts.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Zumba by Tiny Fitness
item.description:
Sunday, July 5, 2026 at 9 AM

Join the party and sweat with Tiny Fitness to rhythms from around the world! This hour-long Zumba fitness class will have you grooving, shaking, and toning to salsa, hip hop, samba, and bhangra beats – and so many more. Open to all fitness levels and recommended for ages 16+, no equipment needed. Always move to your own beat and consult your health team before beginning or modifying your exercise program. To learn more about the Tiny Fitness studio visit their website.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Free Watch Party: Round of 16 (W81 vs W82)
item.description:
Monday, July 6, 2026 at 5:30 PM

The first knock out round is over and the field has been cut in half. Every match from here is win or go home, and tonight someone’s World Cup journey ends. The Round of 16 is knockout soccer at its most raw and thrilling. Be here for it! Festival gates open at 5:30 p.m. to the sounds of DJ Athenz. Kickoff is at 7:00 p.m. Admission is free! Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Visit Smalls Sliders for burgers, fries, and shakes and cool down with Frios Gourmet Pops. Stop by Hearsay’s festival tent for a cold beer or beer bucket or grab a michelada, wine, or beer from Gaspacho’s festival tent. Non-alcoholic drinks from Coca-Cola as well as pre-packaged snacks can be purchased at the Community Partner Concessions Booth, proceeds benefiting Levy Park and the event’s community partners. Community Partners: Supporting this event is the Japan-America Society of Houston. The Japan-America Society of Houston (JASH) is the leading organization that connects Houston’s diverse community, local businesses, and individuals to advance the mutual interest of American and Japanese peoples. JASH programs in language and education, arts and culture, and business initiatives and networking provide opportunities for collaboration and important people-to-people exchange. JASH hosts a monthly program at Levy Park: Japan Junction, as well as seasonal programming throughout the year. All proceeds from the Community Partner Concessions booth will support JASH and Levy Park. Face Painting & Henna Art: Get into the tournament spirit with face and body art from Gio’s Art Kreations and Henna Station & Glitter Bar Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds. Festival Guidelines: VISITORS MUST COMPLY WITH DIRECTIONS FROM PARK PERSONNEL, OR THEY WILL BE ASKED TO LEAVE THE PARK. PARENTS ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THEIR CHILDREN’S SAFETY IN THE PARK. CHAIRS AND SEATING: No outside furniture. Blankets may be brought into the Festival site for the designated blanket seating area. All other furniture is available on a first-come, first-served basis. STROLLERS & WAGONS: Oversized strollers and wagons may need to be parked in a designated area during the festival. All visitors must comply with park personnel regarding stroller and wagon parking. FOOD AND BEVERAGES: Guests are permitted to enter with one factory-sealed bottle of water. Outside food and drinks are prohibited (exceptions will be made for baby food and necessary medical dietary food items). A variety of food, non-alcoholic drinks, beer, and wine will be available for purchase from Festival concessionaires. RE-ENTRY: If you leave the Festival site, you will be re-screened before re-entry. THE FOLLOWING ARE PROHIBITED: Smoking or Vaping Umbrellas Drug Use Kites and Drones Recreational Motorized Vehicles Guns, knives, any weaponry Soliciting Bikes, scooters, rollerblades, and roller skates

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

Join the party and sweat with Tiny Fitness to rhythms from around the world! This hour-long Zumba fitness class will have you grooving, shaking, and toning to salsa, hip hop, samba, and bhangra beats – and so many more. Open to all fitness levels and recommended for ages 16+, no equipment needed. Always move to your own beat and consult your health team before beginning or modifying your exercise program. Monday, July 6, 2026 at 6 PM

From McGonigel's Mucky Duck

Event details

item.title: Family Storytime & Craft
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Join us for a cross-generational literacy experience! Family Storytime features a story followed by a craft that accompanies the reading. All craft supplies are included as a part of this free program.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Texas Sound Meditation with Leala and James
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 7 PM

Levy Park brings “Self Care” to a whole new level with Sound Healing led by Leala and James. A sound bath is a gentle, nurturing, and cathartic meditation experience for anyone looking to improve the quality of their life. This act of self-care is beneficial for anyone from beginners to advanced meditators who are ready to experience a greater sense of peace and well-being. The Levy Park Sound Baths will assist in clearing out that which is blocking the natural vitality while strengthening the connection and balance between mind, body, and soul. A collectively cultivated safe space offers participants an opportunity to deepen their own journey of self-love, healing or transformation. While relieving stress, tension and anxiety this sound immersion experience will have you feeling revitalized, relaxed and rejuvenate. Let the sounds wash away everything that no longer serves you as you set your intentions and manifest your desires! * Formerly Astral.WAVZ Sound Therapy

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Magnolia Brothers
item.description:
Tuesday, July 7, 2026 at 7 PM

From McGonigel's Mucky Duck

Event details

item.title: Japan Junction
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

June 10: “The Tubmaker Who Flew to the Sea” and Teru Teru Bozu paper craft June in Japan is known as Tsuyu, the rainy season, when gentle rains and cloudy skies become a part of daily life. But what do you do when you are hoping for sunny weather before an important day? In Japan, people often hang Teru Teru Bozu, a traditional handmade decoration believed to bring good weather. Usually made from tissue paper or cloth, these cheerful little figures are displayed near windows or doors to wish for sunshine. Join us for a kamishibai reading of “Okeya no Ten Nobori” or “The Tubmaker Who Flew to the Sky,” a whimsical tale featuring a sleepy thunder god, Kaminari-sama, whose storms bring rainy weather. After the story, make your own Teru Teru Bozu out of craft paper to chase away the rain and Kaminari-sama and invite sunny skies!

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Bollywood & Bhangra Dance
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Get ready to move, sweat, and dance in this fitness-based dance class inspired by the vibrant rhythms of Bollywood and the powerful beats of Bhangra. Designed for all ages, abilities, and experience levels, this class welcomes everyone — whether you’re stepping onto the dance floor for the first time or already love to dance.      

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Slow Flow with Stephanie McCarrey
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 7 PM

This is an all-levels yoga flow for beginners to experts that will help you build strength and mobility. The vinyasa flow is set to the beat of the latest top 40 and throwback favorites. The last portion of class will be all about opening the body and increasing mobility.  Each class will end with a sound bath Shavasana.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Free Watch Party: Quarterfinals Day 1
item.description:
Thursday, July 9, 2026 at 1:30 PM

The group stage drama is behind us — now it’s pure knockout soccer. Eight teams left standing, and tonight two of them go to war for a spot in the semifinals. Come feel the electricity. Kickoff is at 3:00 p.m. Admission is free! Community Partners: Further enhancing programming, Levy Park will partner with local organizations to broaden community engagement, including collaborations with cultural groups and soccer-focused nonprofits. Levy Park is seeking enthusiastic volunteers and community partner organizations to help bring this festival to life. Volunteers will work directly alongside our event team, supporting booth operations and helping ensure every guest has an unforgettable experience. Email us to learn more about volunteer opportunities. Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Children’s Bingo
item.description:
Thursday, July 9, 2026 at 4:30 PM

Bring the kiddos and join our Levy Park team for Children’s Bingo! This 30-minute program, ideal for kids aged 4 to 12, is a fun way for children to practice their letters and numbers while earning prizes. Levy Park provides Bingo cards, markers, and prizes. B-I-N-G-O!

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Blue Dragon Tai Chi
item.description:
Thursday, July 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Join us at Levy Park with Taoist Healing for Tai Chi and Meditation every Friday at 9 AM and every Thursday 6 PM. Practice Tai Chi to connect and focus the body and mind with slow, low-impact gentle movements and stretches. Open to all ages and levels, this class is great for those who want to de-stress, find their inner peace, and increase overall health.

From Levy Park Houston

The last portion of class will be all about opening the body and increasing mobility.  Each class will end with a sound bath Shavasana.

From Levy Park Houston

Practice Tai Chi to connect and focus the body and mind with slow, low-impact gentle movements and stretches. Open to all ages and levels, this class is great for those who want to de-stress, find their inner peace, and increase overall health.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Free Watch Party: Quarterfinals Day 2
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Yesterday, someone’s dream ended. Tonight, two more teams leave everything on the pitch for a chance to write their name in World Cup history. We’ll be watching — will you? Kickoff is at 2:00 p.m. Admission is free! Community Partner: We are proud to be joined by our Community Partner, Dynamo & Dash Charities, for this event! By leveraging the passion of players, staff, and fans, Dynamo & Dash Charities provides resources and education to positively impact lives in the community. Stop by the Community Partner Booth to support their amazing work and learn more about their initiatives. All food & beverage sale proceeds from the Community Partner booth will support Dynamo & Dash Charities and Levy Park. Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Sunset Bingo at Levy Park
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Bring your crew the second Friday of each month for a fun evening where you can take home fantastic prizes. Set to the backdrop of soulful tunes from DJ Del Sur, Sunset Bingo is a great way to unwind and support Levy Park. All event proceeds directly support the park’s operations and programming. Please note: While children (and pups) can play, an adult (18+) must be present to claim a prize. $10 for 8 games; $1 daubers are available for purchase, or BYODauber (bring your own dauber). CASH ONLY Event Schedule: 6 p.m. – Board Sales Begin 7 p.m. – Games Begin Food & Beverage Concessions: Enjoy convenient counter service with easy-to-eat, handheld favorites like tortas, flautas, hot dogs, and tacos at Gaspachos Mexican Bites—plus special treats for your four-legged friends! Hearsay is also serving up its eclectic cuisine, refreshing cocktails, and treats for your pup. Kind Ice Cream offers a 10% discount during games, allowing Bingo participants to enjoy its irresistible selection of cakes and ice cream treats. Outside alcohol is not permitted but may be purchased from park concessionaires. No outside furniture is allowed. Park furniture will be available, or guests may bring a blanket for lawn seating. Parking Parking is available adjacent to the park and along Eastside Street and Wakeforest Avenue. Additional public parking (parking rate applies) is available in the Kirby Grove parking garage located on the corner of Richmond and Wakeforest. Please note: Prizes and sponsors may vary by event. If you would like to donate an in-kind prize or sponsor Sunset Bingo, please contact us at info@levyparkhouston.org.  

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

Location: Levy Park (Event Pavilion), TX

Designed for all ages, abilities, and experience levels, this class welcomes everyone — whether you’re stepping onto the dance floor for the first time or already love to dance.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Houston Grand Opera- Storybook Opera
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 11 AM

Storytelling like you’ve never heard it before takes center stage as an opera singer presents an engaging and enjoyable introduction to opera for students. This program features singing storytellers for 30 minutes of captivating fun! 4/4 – Lula the Mighty Griot 5/2 – Eyes That Meet at the Corner 6/6 – Goldilocks and the 3 Bears 7/11 – World Cup Women 9/5 – TBD 10/3 – TBD 11/7 –  TBD 12/5 – TBD  

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Dance and Move with Young Audiences of Houston
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 1 PM

Participants will learn to combine elements in dance, music, and theatre while expressing themselves through creative movement. We will use the fundamentals of dance to focus on story-telling while building skills in self-awareness, social awareness, relationship skills, and responsible decision making.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details

item.title: Quarterfinals Double Header
item.description:
Saturday, July 11, 2026 at 2:30 PM

Three days of quarterfinal madness wrap up tonight, and the stakes couldn’t be higher. By the final whistle, we’ll know our four semifinalists. Don’t miss the moment it all becomes clear. Kickoff for Game 1 is at 4:00 p.m. Kickoff for Game 2 is at 8:00 p.m. Admission is free! Community Partners: Further enhancing programming, Levy Park will partner with local organizations to broaden community engagement, including collaborations with cultural groups and soccer-focused nonprofits. Levy Park is seeking enthusiastic volunteers and community partner organizations to help bring this festival to life. Volunteers will work directly alongside our event team, supporting booth operations and helping ensure every guest has an unforgettable experience. Email us to learn more about volunteer opportunities. Food and Beverage Concessions: Enjoy a variety of festival food fare, beer and wine, and non-alcoholic beverages from Coca-Cola. Merchandise: Event merchandise celebrating Houston, Levy Park, and the global soccer tournament will also be available for purchase. Art Installations and Photo Opportunities: Commemorate the experience with a photo tour of the park, including life-sized soccer-themed photo backdrops and a tournament bracket. Visit our soccer ball music box and turn the crank for festive sounds.

From Levy Park Houston

Event details
